claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 18 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child. I'm inquiring about my son Alex, who's 18 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I'd like to ask about Alex's current situation.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, is he at school, college, sixth form, or doing an approved training course?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. He's in sixth form doing his A-levels.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. 

**Do you live in the UK, and does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Are you currently the person receiving Child Benefit for Alex, or is someone else claiming it for him?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. I'm not currently receiving it, which is why I'm inquiring about whether I'm eligible.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. One final question:

**Has Alex started any paid work for 24 hours or more per week, or is he receiving any benefits in his own right such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't work 24 hours or more per week, and he's not receiving any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:

- Alex is 18 years old and is in approved education (sixth form doing A-levels)
- You can continue to receive Child Benefit for children under 20 who remain in approved education or training
- You live in the UK and Alex lives with you, so you are responsible for him
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- Alex is not working 24+ hours per week or receiving benefits in his own right

You meet all the eligibility criteria, so you can apply for Child Benefit for Alex. The benefit can continue as long as he remains in approved education or training and he's under 20 years old.

If you haven't already claimed, you should make a claim for Child Benefit. Please note that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and is currently in sixth form doing his A-levels, which qualifies as approved education. Under the Child Benefit rules, you normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. You live in the UK and Alex lives with you, which means you are responsible for him. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and he is not working 24 hours or more per week, nor is he receiving benefits in his own right (such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit). Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their son Alex. Alex is 18 years old and in approved education (sixth form doing A-levels), which allows Child Benefit to continue until age 20. The claimant lives in the UK with Alex, is responsible for him, no one else is claiming for him, and Alex is not working 24+ hours per week or claiming benefits in his own right. All eligibility criteria are met.

Under the Child Benefit rules, you normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. You live in the UK and Alex lives with you, which means you are responsible for him. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and he is not working 24 hours or more per week, nor is he receiving benefits in his own right (such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit). Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their son Alex. Alex is 18 years old and in approved education (sixth form doing A-levels), which allows Child Benefit to continue until age 20. The claimant lives in the UK with Alex, is responsible for him, no one else is claiming for him, and Alex is not working 24+ hours per week or claiming benefits in his own right. All eligibility criteria are met.
